intent: Calibrate discovery quality against explicit positive, negative and boundary examples before tightening ranking or policy defaults further.
evaluationQuestions: Landen civic/public-event/source-family Repos sichtbar vor generischen framework- oder dashboard-Treffern?
Werden offene Event-Intake-, Adapter- und Normalization-Muster bevorzugt statt bloßer Kalender- oder UI-Ähnlichkeit?
Bleiben Grenzfälle sichtbar genug für Audit, ohne die eigentliche Topliste zu dominieren?

positiveRepos.why: Public-event intake plus scraper-family signal with civic/open-data relevance.
Source-family and civic scraping baseline for adapter and intake patterns.

negativeRepos.why: Framework/UI noise and not target-relevant for EventBear worker core.
Tutorial/framework noise with no meaningful public-event or source-family grounding.
Generic list-style repo that should never rank as a top EventBear discovery hit.

boundaryRepos.why: Potentially useful agenda/scraper signal, but not clearly civic-source-family enough to become the default top hit.
Calendar/event-like but vertical-specific and should stay below stronger civic/public-event infra signals.
